text.title: Agent Rules and GAQL Gotchas for Reporting
text.summary: Concrete rules for reporting agents and LLM failure modes specific to Google Ads reporting and optimization.

rule.statement: All cost fields are in micros (1 unit = $0.000001); divide by 1,000,000 before display. Apply this to cost_micros, cost_per_conversion, and average_cpc.
rule.rationale: Presenting raw micros as currency values produces numbers 1,000,000x too large. Alert thresholds set in dollars will fire on every campaign if the agent forgets to convert.

rule.statement: metrics.conversions is a double (float), not an integer; values like 0.5 represent fractional attribution. Do not cast to int.
rule.rationale: Fractional attribution from data-driven model assigns partial conversion credit. Truncating to int silently understates conversions and overstates CPA.

rule.statement: metrics.search_impression_share is a fraction between 0 and 1; multiply by 100 to display as a percentage.
rule.rationale: Displaying 0.45 as "45%" is correct. Displaying it as "0.45%" is wrong and will cause users to incorrectly believe impression share is near zero.

antipattern.description: Generating GAQL queries with field names not in the Google Ads API (e.g., metrics.spend, metrics.budget).
antipattern.reason: Non-existent field names cause query failures with generic INVALID_ARGUMENT errors. Agents must validate field names against the API reference for the pinned version.

rule.statement: Use metrics.conversions for ROAS and CPA calculations; use metrics.all_conversions only when the explicit goal is to count all micro-conversion actions.
rule.rationale: all_conversions includes every conversion action regardless of whether it is marked "include in Conversions." Using it for ROAS inflates the metric and gives false efficiency signals.

rule.statement: Do not assume search_term_view provides complete coverage; terms below a volume floor aggregate to "(other)" — often 30-40% of spend. Acknowledge this gap in any report.
rule.rationale: Acting as if search term data is complete leads to over-confidence in negative keyword coverage. Significant spend flows through "(other)" that no amount of triage can address.

list.item: Pausing any campaign: human notification required before execution
Adding/removing keywords with spend impact above $X threshold: human approval
Bid strategy change: human approval required
Changes touching more than 10% of active campaigns in one run: human approval
